feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: 5 Habits to Start Now for Healthier Aging, According to Oxford Scientists
entry.summary: Oxford scientists reveal 5 habits for healthy aging: sleep, mindset, exercise, diet and stress. Learn the SMEDs acronym and practical wellness tips.

entry.title: College Student Adjusts Schedule After Roommate Complains About Her Going Out at Night
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: First-of-its-kind UCSF study shows blood test may catch early cognitive trouble
entry.summary: Alzheimer's disease remains a top cause of death in the U.S. and cases are expected to almost double by 2060.

entry.title: Ozempic may be reshaping the brain, scientists say
entry.summary: Scientists are exploring how GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic may not only be a metabolism breakthrough, but could also reshape some patients’ brains. GLP-1s were originally developed to treat diabetes and obesity, but researchers at the University of Colorado Anschu…

entry.title: Skip the Protein Bars—Dietitians Recommend These 9 High-Protein, High-Fiber Snacks Instead
entry.summary: We asked dietitians for the best protein- and fiber-packed snacks that aren't a boring protein bar. Here are the delicious and nutritious snacks they recommend.

entry.title: Health department issues warning after rabid bat found inside home, resident exposed in McHenry County
entry.summary: Health officials said that a resident was exposed to a bat, which tested positive for rabies, that was found in a house in McHenry County, the first rabid bat discovery in the county this year.

entry.title: Titanium particles may explain why antibiotics fail against dental implant infections
entry.summary: Dental implants have given tens of millions of people something dentures never could: a full set of fixed and fully functioning teeth. Unfortunately, 10% to 20% of implant patients eventually experience an aggressive jawbone infection called peri-implantitis.…

entry.title: 4 Simple Rules for a Healthy Gut
entry.summary: The microbiota in your digestive system plays a role in metabolism, mood, and even your genes. Here’s how to optimize it for a happy gut.

entry.title: NY's first case of tick-borne Bourbon virus confirmed on Long Island
entry.summary: "We are just seeing the tip of the iceberg," said one Stony Brook doctor.

entry.title: Why Noninvasive Blood Glucose Monitoring Is Still the Holy Grail of Wearables
entry.summary: Glucose is an incredibly annoying bodily component to work with.

entry.title: Experts Warn These Stomach Symptoms Could Signal Something Serious
entry.summary: Discover the signs your upset stomach isn't normal, including when you should make an appointment to see a doctor about your symptoms.

entry.title: Vibrio season ramping up as 5 Floridians struck by horrific infection
entry.summary: The 'fleshing-eating' disease caused by the Vibrio vulnificus bacterium is expected to increase with rising oceanic temperatures.

entry.title: As RFK Jr. targets antidepressants, WA residents share their experiences
entry.summary: In Washington, 13.6% of adults say they currently take antidepressants, and an additional 17% say they have taken them previously.

entry.title: Forget LASIK: Safer, cheaper vision correction without lasers or surgery
entry.summary: Researchers are developing a futuristic alternative to LASIK that reshapes the eye without lasers or incisions. Using mild electrical pulses and platinum contact lenses, they temporarily soften the cornea so it can be molded into a new shape. Early tests on r…

entry.title: Calcium Vs. Magnesium: Doctors Reveal Which Is Better for Bone Health
entry.summary: Doctors compare the importance of calcium vs. magnesium for bone health, including which is more important as you age and key supplement interactions to know.

entry.title: Pollen is out of control around New England right now. Here’s why.
entry.summary: Allergy season across the region has surged after a very quiet start this spring.

entry.title: Brain scans of new fathers show measurable changes — which might explain why so many dads describe the first year of parenthood as feeling like learning to be a different person
entry.summary: Ask a father about the first year after his child was born, and a particular phrase tends to surface eventually. Not the sleeplessness, not the logistics, not the specific chaos of keeping a newborn alive — though all of those come up too. What comes up, give…

entry.title: 'It's being promoted like there's absolutely no risk': Why some experts say melatonin should be considered a drug rather than a supplement
entry.summary: Researchers want stricter testing, better labels and accurate dosages for a wildly popular sleep aid that comes with some risks.

entry.title: Kyle Busch’s Death Exposes The ‘Silent Killer’ Warning Signs Everyone Should Know
entry.summary: At just 41 years old, NASCAR legend Kyle Busch died after severe pneumonia progressed into sepsis, causing fatal complications.In Busch’s case, reports indicate he initially believed he was dealing with a sinus infection or cold before the illness worsened in…

entry.title: Ask Sahaj: I’m 27, have few friends and am still single. I feel so behind.
entry.summary: A letter writer can’t help “but feel wistful at how my life looks nothing like I hoped it would today.”

entry.title: The 1 Undiscussed Illness That Spikes During The Summer, According To Doctors
entry.summary: You don’t need to give up your favorite summer activities to stay healthy, but a few precautions can make a big difference.

entry.title: This Is How Much You Need to Walk to Lose Weight
entry.summary: New research suggests that walking less than 10,000 steps a day can help keep weight off. Here's why.

entry.title: 4 minutes of exercise a day could help control blood sugar
entry.summary: A new study found short “exercise snacks” were practical and effective for people with Type 2 diabetes.

entry.title: These thyroid disorder symptoms are easy to mistake for normal aging
entry.summary: Thyroid problems can mimic other health conditions and hormonal changes, leading people to brush off symptoms and delay treatment.

entry.title: Psilocybin research is no longer just for hard-to-treat cases — a new trial targeted recurrent depression in people who had not failed standard treatment, and the results are promising
entry.summary: Most of the psilocybin research that has made headlines over the past decade has carried an implicit qualifier: this is for people for whom nothing else has worked. The participants enrolled in those studies typically had treatment-resistant depression — a co…

entry.title: Everyone is suddenly worried about cortisol spikes. So I asked doctors to weigh in on what's real.
entry.summary: Cortisol is the wellness industry's latest villain, blamed for stress, weight gain, and poor sleep. Doctors say most people shouldn't panic.

entry.title: The Supplement That Science Says Could Make the World Less Angry
entry.summary: If you found out a supplement existed that could lower aggression, you would probably think it was something that would sedate you, like a sleep aid. In real...

entry.title: 8 common food additives linked to high blood pressure in new study
entry.summary: In a study involving more than 100,000 participants, researchers found that 8 food preservatives could harm cardiovascular health and were linked to a higher risk of hypertension.

entry.title: Scientists thought brain inflammation was driving long COVID but the scans told a different story
entry.summary: A new brain imaging study has found no evidence of widespread brain inflammation in patients suffering from prolonged symptoms after COVID-19 infection. Instead, the most severe long COVID symptoms were associated with increased brain activity in regions invo…

entry.title: Rotavirus levels rising across Bay Area: Here's how it spreads, who's most at risk
entry.summary: High concentrations of rotavirus across the Bay Area are raising concerns among infectious disease experts. Here's what to know.

entry.title: The World Cup Is a Petri Dish
entry.summary: Millions of international visitors are coming to North America for the competition, and the odds are that quite a few will be unwell.

entry.title: Anger and suspicion as Ebola spreads through eastern DR Congo
entry.summary: Disinformation and mistrust have hampered efforts to contain third-worst outbreak of virus in history

entry.title: “Will Actively Hunt People”: 65 Scary Science Facts That Might Change How You See The World
entry.summary: We should all strive to be curious, open-minded, and seek to get as close to the objective truth as possible. However, this doesn’t change the fact that, well, the truth can often be pretty uncomfortable. You need a lot of emotional resilience to live with th…

entry.title: Aid cuts and climate change drive deadly malaria surge in Zimbabwe
entry.summary: A surge in malaria cases in Zimbabwe is exposing fragile health systems and growing treatment shortages in rural areas.

entry.title: Raccoon tests positve for rabies in Chapel Hill
entry.summary: Orange County Animal Services said two dogs were having an altercation with a raccoon on Saturday, May 23, near East Franklin Street and Glendale Drive in Chapel Hill.

entry.title: Do Multivitamins Actually Make a Difference? What a Pharmacist Says
entry.summary: Multivitamins can support normal growth and function in the body, but not everyone needs to take them daily to support overall health.

entry.title: 3 Things Women Should Do to Lower Their Dementia Risk, According to Science
entry.summary: Learn three science-backed steps women can take to lower their dementia risk. Find out key modifiable factors to help protect your cognitive health.

entry.title: California hikers hit with suspected norovirus outbreak
entry.summary: One hiker had to be airlifted from the trail for medical care.

entry.title: Scientists Just Connected This Vitamin to Surprising Gut Health Benefits
entry.summary: Scientists found vitamin D supports gut health and reduces inflammation in people with IBD. Learn how vitamin D impacts your gut and immune system.

entry.title: What Clinicians Should Know about Ebola Bundibugyo Virus
entry.summary: What Clinicians Should Know about Ebola Bundibugyo Virus

entry.title: Wisconsin DHS confirms 5 mpox cases
entry.summary: MADISON (WKOW) — The Wisconsin Department of Health Services (DHS) confirmed five mpox cases in the state this year, with a cluster identified in southeastern Wisconsin and cases in northern

entry.title: One Weight Loss Strategy Is 5x More Effective Than Ozempic, Study Shows
entry.summary: Ozempic and other semaglutide medications have been working wonders in terms of shedding pounds for many people.

entry.title: Distinct genetic architecture in the tails of complex traits
entry.summary: Genome-wide analyses in multiancestry and European cohorts show that in complex traits, rare alleles have disproportionately large effects at the tails of the phenotypic spectrum compared with common alleles.

entry.title: αKG-mediated carnitine synthesis drives DNA repair via histone acetylation
entry.summary: The metabolite αKG promotes carnitine synthesis and increases site-specific histone acetylation, thereby promoting homologous recombination-mediated DNA repair, which has potential implications for chemoresistant cancers.

entry.title: After years of long COVID, Seattle father and former teacher turns to UW Medicine for help
entry.summary: Jesse Hagopian meets a lot of people through a computer screen these days. The former Garfield High School teacher, musician, coach and father of two is one of

entry.title: What Happens When You Drink an Olive Oil and Lemon Shot Every Morning?
entry.summary: Olive oil and lemon juice each offer health benefits like healthy fats, antioxidants, and vitamin C, but there isn't strong evidence that combining them crea...

entry.title: Blood testing is now included in screening recommendations for colon and rectal cancer
entry.summary: The American Cancer Society is adding some new testing options to its screening guideline for colorectal cancers – and for the first time, that includes a blood test.

entry.title: Why caffeine can sabotage deep sleep even when you still get eight hours
entry.summary: Evening coffee has sparked controversy for years. Some people fall asleep without difficulty, while others toss and turn for half the night. However, a growing body of research suggests the question of whether coffee makes it harder to fall asleep may be too …

entry.title: My Patient Invited Me To Her Home To Watch Her Die. What I Saw When I Arrived Gave Me Goosebumps.
entry.summary: “She sat down and paused for a few seconds, and then looked at me earnestly and asked, ‘Will you be there to hold my hand?’”

entry.title: Gene-expression patterns can be used to estimate mortality risk and chronological age
entry.summary: Massive analyses of RNA transcripts from rodents, monkeys and humans reveal hallmarks of ageing that could expedite the development of anti-ageing interventions.
